channel.title: Docker on Cédric Walter blog
channel.description: Recent content in Docker on Cédric Walter blog
channel.copyright: Reproduction without explicit permission is prohibited. All Rights Reserved. All photos remain copyright © their rightful owners. No copyright infringement is intended.

item.title: Dynamic IP at home, no problem with ddclient and Cloudflare
item.description: ddclient is an open-source Perl-based client used to update dynamic DNS entries for various DDNS service providers. It supports a wide range of protocols, including DynDNS, No-IP, DuckDNS, Cloudflare, and many others, making it a flexible choice for users with diverse needs.

item.title: Reverse proxy Docker applications and applications with Let's Encrypt and Traefik
item.description: Securing Docker containers with Let’s Encrypt and Traefik involves setting up a reverse proxy with SSL termination using Traefik and obtaining SSL/TLS certificates with the help of letsencrypt

item.title: OpenVPN server in a Docker container
item.description: OpenVPN server in a Docker container complete with an EasyRSA PKI CA.

item.title: Securing a Docker registry with Let's Encrypt and Nginx-proxy
item.description: Securing Docker containers with Let’s Encrypt and Nginx-proxy-manager involves setting up a reverse proxy with SSL termination using nginx-proxy and obtaining SSL/TLS certificates with the help of letsencrypt

item.title: Homelab - dont rely on services where YOU are the product
item.description: A homelab, short for home laboratory, refers to a setup where individuals or hobbyists create a small-scale computing or networking environment in their homes for various purposes. The goal is typically to gain hands-on experience, test and learn about different technologies, and build skills related to IT, networking, server administration, and more. Homelabs can vary widely in complexity and purpose, depending on the interests and goals of the individual setting them up!
